channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At least 10 killed at adult education center in what officials say is Sweden's worst mass shooting
item.description: At least 10 people, including the gunman, were killed Tuesday at an adult education center in what Sweden’s prime minister called the country’s worst mass shooting. But a final death toll, a conclusive number of wounded and a motive hadn’t yet been determined hours later.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump withdraws from 2 UN bodies, orders review of UNESCO
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday signed an executive order withdrawing the U.S. from the United Nations Human Rights Council and from participating in UNRWA, the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees. Trump also directed the U.S. to review its participation in the U.N.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump’s tariff tactics carry higher economic risks than during his first term
item.description: When Donald Trump started the biggest trade war since the 1930s in his first term, his impulsive combination of threats and import taxes on U.S. trading partners created chaos, generated drama -- and drew criticism from mainstream economists who favor free trade.
item.dc:creator: Paul Wiseman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jewish population in West Bank keeps rising. Settlers hope Trump will accelerate growth
item.description: The Jewish population in the West Bank grew at twice the rate of the general Israeli population last year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What to know about El Salvador's mega-prison after Trump deal to send people there
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — The Trump administration and the president of El Salvador said Monday that they'd struck a deal allowing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As hundreds of quakes rattle Greek islands, a few brave tourists have Santorini to themselves
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — The hundreds of earthquakes that have rattled Greek islands on the Aegean Sea are a “seismic swarm" and could continue for weeks before eventually diminishing, the Santorini mayor told The Associated Press on Tuesday — pausing to chuckle as new tremors shook items on his des
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: El Salvador agrees to accept US deportees of any nationality following meeting with Rubio
item.description: El Salvador's President Nayib Bukele has offered to accept convicted criminals of any nationality the U.S. wants to deport and house them in his country's prison system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Modi's ruling party looks to topple corruption crusader in Delhi poll with graft allegations
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The resurgent Hindu nationalist party of India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi is eyeing a state legislature election in Delhi where it is looking to unseat the governing movement which has led the Indian capital for over a decade but has recently been embroiled in graft allegations.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Hostages freed from Gaza rarely saw sunlight, families say
item.description: The families of hostages freed from Gaza over the weekend, the latest release in the ceasefire deal, described difficult conditions — including rarely seeing the sun — during their captivity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emergency crews deployed on Santorini as an earthquake swarm worries Greek experts
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — Authorities deployed emergency rescue crews to Greece's volcanic island of Santorini on Monday as hundreds of people scrambled to leave after a spike in seismic activity raised concerns about a potentially powerful earthquake.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cartels targeting Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones, explosives amid Trump crackdown: report
item.description: Mexican drug cartels have planned attacks on U.S. Border Patrol agents with kamikaze drones and other explosives to combat a crackdown by the Trump administration at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Britain's Starmer seeks strong ties with both the EU and Donald Trump. That could be tricky
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Keir Starmer travels to Brussels on Monday, becoming the first British prime minister to attend a European Union leaders’ meeting since the U.K. left the bloc five years ago.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Their sacred land was a gift for their courage. Yet Maká people in Paraguay fight for its ownership
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Many Maká traditions have slowly faded. Yet a few elders among these Paraguayan Indigenous people recall how their songs imitated birds.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Preoccupied with its troops in Russia, North Korea unlikely to embrace Trump's overture soon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In its first direct criticism of the Trump administration, North Korea lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned Monday that such “coarse and nonsensical remarks” will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Belgium has a new prime minister, one who long tried to gut the nation and seek regional autonomy
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Belgium has a new prime minister whose political purpose was long to break up the nation, gut the state structures and give ever more autonomy to his northern Flanders at the cost of everyone else.
item.dc:creator: RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump urged to look into US funding of Lebanese army amid accusations of its ties to Hezbollah
item.description: Amid a resurgence of Hezbollah activity on Israel’s border there are new calls for the Trump administration to push the Lebanese state to root out Hezbollah and slash aid to the country’s army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama pledges to end key canal deal with China, work with US after Rubio visit
item.description: Panama's José Raúl Mulino announced that his country would not renew its agreement with China's Belt and Road Initiative following a visit from Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EU warns Trump it would ‘respond firmly’ to tariffs
item.description: “The EU would respond firmly to any trading partner that unfairly or arbitrarily imposes tariffs on EU goods,” a spokesperson said. “There is a lot at stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arab countries reject Trump proposal to move Palestinians from Gaza into Egypt and Jordan
item.description: Arab countries released a statement opposing President Donald Trump's idea of relocating 1.5 Palestinian refugees from demolished Gaza to Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu departs for US to meet with Trump, hoping to strengthen ties with Washington
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu left for the U.S. on Sunday to meet with U.S. President Donald Trump, looking to strengthen ties with Washington.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: King Charles says 'our hearts' are with United States after 'devastating loss of life' in DC plane crash
item.description: King Charles III said Saturday he was "shocked and saddened" about the deadly midair collision near Reagan National Airport Wednesday, saying his thoughts were with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaza ceasefire sees its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — The ceasefire in Gaza saw its smoothest exchange yet of Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners on Saturday, and the crucial Rafah border crossing reopened two days before discussions on the truce’s far more difficult second phase begin.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and IMAD ISSEID, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela frees 6 Americans after meeting between President Maduro and Trump's envoy
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Six Americans who had been detained in Venezuela in recent months were freed by the government of President Nicolás Maduro after he met Friday with a Trump administration official tasked with urging the authoritarian leader to take back deported migrants who have committed
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he ordered airstrikes on Islamic State in Somalia
item.description: "These killers, who we found hiding in caves, threatened the United States and our Allies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Rubio heads to Panama, Latin America to pursue Trump's 'Golden Age' agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is set to visit Panama in his first official visit abroad as part of what the State Department said is to underscore President Donald Trump's vision of re-establishing a "Golden Era.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump uniquely placed to ‘whisper’ in Erdogan’s ear over Turkish regional ambitions: Greek defense minister
item.description: Greek Defense Minister Nikos Dendias said NATO needs to take seriously Turkey's expansionist ambitions and President Erdoğan's desire to "recreate the Ottoman Empire" amid aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — NASA’s two stuck astronauts took their first spacewalk together Thursday, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Investigators recover black boxes from plane in DC crash
item.description: There were 60 passengers and four crew members aboard the plane, which was arriving from Wichita, Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
